description: Add typed messages (text, query, response, handoff, broadcast) with optional metadata, reply_to threading, and a /broadcast endpoint to claude-peers broker. Depends on broker auth (prd-broker-auth.md) being implemented first — all new endpoints require Authorization: Bearer <token>.

userStories.title: Type definitions for structured messages and broadcast
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MessageType, updated Message/SendMessageRequest interfaces, and new BroadcastRequest/BroadcastResponse interfaces in shared/types.ts so that all downstream code has correct types to import
acceptanceCriteria: MessageType is defined as 'text' | 'query' | 'response' | 'handoff' | 'broadcast' in shared/types.ts
Message interface has three new fields: type: MessageType (required), metadata: Record<string, unknown> | null (nullable), reply_to: number | null (nullable)
SendMessageRequest interface has three new optional fields: type?: MessageType, metadata?: Record<string, unknown>, reply_to?: number
New BroadcastRequest interface with from_id, text, type?, metadata?, scope ('machine'|'directory'|'repo'), cwd, git_root
New BroadcastResponse interface with ok: boolean, recipients: number, message_ids: number[]
Existing type exports are not broken — broker.ts and server.ts still import without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story is not in the original PRD as a standalone story — it is extracted from FR-1 through FR-5. Must be completed before all other stories.

userStories.title: Database migration for new message columns
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the database schema migration to add type, metadata, and reply_to columns to the messages table without data loss so that existing databases upgrade seamlessly
acceptanceCriteria: Three ALTER TABLE statements execute on broker startup: ADD COLUMN type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'text', ADD COLUMN metadata TEXT DEFAULT NULL, ADD COLUMN reply_to INTEGER DEFAULT NULL
Each ALTER is wrapped in try/catch so column-already-exists is a no-op
Existing messages get type='text', metadata=NULL, reply_to=NULL via column defaults
The CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS messages statement includes all three new columns for fresh databases
No data migration script needed — defaults handle existing rows
Broker starts without error on a database that already has the columns
Broker starts without error on a database that lacks the columns
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same pattern as session_name migration on line 61 of broker.ts. Maps to PRD US-007.

userStories.title: Broker handleSendMessage update for structured fields
userStories.description: As a Claude Code session, I want to send messages with type, metadata, and reply_to fields so that the broker persists them in SQLite
acceptanceCriteria: insertMessage prepared statement updated to include type, metadata (as JSON string), and reply_to columns
handleSendMessage validates type against the closed set ['text','query','response','handoff','broadcast'] — returns { ok: false, error: 'Invalid message type: ...' } for invalid values
When type is omitted, it defaults to 'text'
metadata must be a valid JSON object or null/omitted — arrays, strings, numbers rejected with { ok: false, error: 'metadata must be a JSON object' }
metadata is stored as JSON.stringify() in SQLite
Combined size of text + JSON.stringify(metadata) must not exceed 10KB — returns { ok: false, error: 'Message too large (text + metadata max 10KB)' } if exceeded
When reply_to is provided, broker validates the referenced message ID exists — returns { ok: false, error: 'Referenced message <id> not found' } if not
reply_to value is stored in messages table
Existing calls to /send-message that omit type, metadata, and reply_to continue to work identically
New selectMessageExists prepared statement: SELECT id FROM messages WHERE id = ?
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Maps to PRD US-001, US-003 (broker parts), and US-008. FR-6, FR-7.

userStories.title: Broker /broadcast endpoint
userStories.description: As a Claude Code session, I want to broadcast a message to all active peers in a given scope so that I can coordinate across sessions without sending individual messages
acceptanceCriteria: New POST /broadcast endpoint accepts { from_id, text, type?, metadata?, scope, cwd, git_root }
handleBroadcast resolves recipients using handleListPeers logic with provided scope/cwd/git_root, excluding from_id
All message inserts are wrapped in a single SQLite transaction for atomicity
Response: { ok: true, recipients: <count>, message_ids: [<ids>] }
Dead peers (failed PID liveness) are skipped, not errored
If no live peers in scope (excluding sender), returns { ok: true, recipients: 0, message_ids: [] }
Type defaults to 'broadcast' when not specified by caller
Caller can override type (e.g., broadcast a 'query' to all peers)
Message size limit: text + JSON.stringify(metadata) must not exceed 10KB
/broadcast is rate-limited as 1 request (same bucket as /send-message) — broadcast to 50 peers counts as 1, not 50
/broadcast route added to the switch statement in Bun.serve fetch handler
/broadcast requires auth token (same pattern as /send-message)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Maps to PRD US-004. FR-8, FR-9. Reuses handleListPeers for scope filtering.

userStories.title: MCP send_message tool update for structured fields
userStories.description: As a Claude Code session, I want the send_message MCP tool to accept optional type, metadata, and reply_to parameters so that I can send structured messages through the MCP interface
acceptanceCriteria: send_message MCP tool inputSchema adds optional 'type' param (string enum: text/query/response/handoff/broadcast)
send_message MCP tool inputSchema adds optional 'metadata' param (object type)
send_message MCP tool inputSchema adds optional 'reply_to' param (number type)
New params are NOT in the required array (backward compatible)
Tool handler passes type, metadata, reply_to through to the broker /send-message endpoint when provided
When params are omitted, behavior is identical to current implementation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Maps to PRD US-001 (MCP tool parts). FR-12.

userStories.title: Channel notifications and check_messages include structured fields
userStories.description: As a Claude Code session receiving messages, I want to see message type, metadata, and reply_to in channel notifications and check_messages output so that I can respond appropriately
acceptanceCriteria: pollAndPushMessages channel notification meta includes type, metadata (parsed from JSON string), and reply_to alongside existing fields
check_messages MCP tool output includes type, metadata, and reply_to for each message
Messages with type 'query' display as: [QUERY] From <name> (<id>): <text>
Messages with type 'handoff' display as: [HANDOFF] From <name> (<id>): <text> followed by task details from metadata
Messages with type 'response' display as: [RESPONSE] From <name> (<id>) re: msg#<reply_to>: <text>
Messages with type 'broadcast' display as: [BROADCAST] From <name> (<id>): <text>
Messages with type 'text' display identically to current format (no prefix) — backward compatible
metadata is parsed from JSON string to object before inclusion in notification meta
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Maps to PRD US-002. FR-10, FR-11.

userStories.title: broadcast_message MCP tool
userStories.description: As a Claude Code session, I want a broadcast_message MCP tool so that I can broadcast to all peers in a scope without knowing the broker API details
acceptanceCriteria: New MCP tool 'broadcast_message' with parameters: message (string, required), scope (enum: machine/directory/repo, required)
Tool automatically sets type to 'broadcast' and includes cwd (myCwd) and git_root (myGitRoot) from server state
Tool returns 'Broadcast sent to <N> peer(s) in scope <scope>' with recipient count
If no peers exist, returns 'No peers found in scope <scope>. Broadcast not sent.'
Tool is registered in the MCP server's TOOLS array
Tool is described in the MCP server instructions string
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Maps to PRD US-005. FR-13, FR-14.

userStories.title: CLI broadcast command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want a 'bun cli.ts broadcast' command so that I can send broadcasts from the terminal
acceptanceCriteria: New CLI command: bun cli.ts broadcast <scope> <message>
Scope must be one of: machine, directory, repo — invalid scope prints error and exits
CLI reads cwd from process.cwd() and resolves git_root (same logic as server.ts)
Prints 'Broadcast sent to <N> peer(s)' on success
Prints 'No peers in scope <scope>' if recipients is 0
Help text updated to include broadcast command
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tests for structured messages
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests covering message types, metadata validation, reply_to threading, and backward compatibility so that regressions are caught early
acceptanceCriteria: New describe block 'Structured Messages' in broker.test.ts
Test: send message with type 'query' stores and returns type correctly
Test: send message with type 'handoff' and metadata round-trips through JSON serialization correctly
Test: send message with reply_to referencing existing message succeeds and stores reply_to
Test: send message with reply_to referencing nonexistent message returns error
Test: send message with invalid metadata (non-object) returns error
Test: send message without type field defaults to 'text'
Test: send message with invalid type value returns error
All tests include auth token in requests (per broker-auth dependency)
All existing tests (19+ including auth tests) continue to pass
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Maps to PRD US-009 (structured messages portion). Placement: after 'Messaging' describe block, before 'Edge Cases'.

userStories.title: Tests for broadcast
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests covering the /broadcast endpoint, scope filtering, atomicity, and rate limiting so that broadcast regressions are caught early
acceptanceCriteria: New describe block 'Broadcast' in broker.test.ts
Test: broadcast to machine scope reaches all peers except sender
Test: broadcast returns correct recipients count and message_ids array
Test: broadcast with scope 'directory' only reaches same-directory peers
Test: broadcast with scope 'repo' only reaches same-repo peers
Test: broadcast to scope with no other peers returns { ok: true, recipients: 0, message_ids: [] }
Test: broadcast respects 10KB message size limit
Test: broadcast sets type to 'broadcast' by default
Test: broadcast counts as 1 request against rate limit, not N
Third peer PID: spawn Bun.spawn(['sleep','60']) in beforeAll, kill in afterAll
All tests include auth token in requests
All existing tests continue to pass
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Maps to PRD US-009 (broadcast portion). Split from US-009 to keep test stories single-context-window sized. Placement: after 'Structured Messages', before 'Edge Cases'.
